Colchicum species are perennial flowering plants, commonly known as autumn crocus, which produce showy, cup-shaped flowers in late summer to early fall. The health benefits of Colchicum species include their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, although they should be used with caution due to their toxicity. The bioactive constituents of Colchicum species include colchicine, a plant alkaloid with anti-inflammatory and cell division-inhibiting properties, as well as other alkaloids and saponins. Colchicum species are traditionally used to make herbal preparations, such as tinctures and infusions, which are used to treat conditions like gout and arthritis, although these preparations should only be used under the guidance of a healthcare professional due to the plant's toxicity.
